teamObjective.explanation: Opening lead with only low non-trump spades. Playing the 3 costs almost nothing and preserves the slightly higher spades for later tricks.

partnerBelief.summary: No specific information about partner's cards beyond the public 3-card count.
evidence: Partner has 3 cards; no cards have been played and no bids were made.

opponentBelief.summary: No specific information about opponent holdings; each opponent has 3 unknown cards.
evidence: No cards have been played yet.

actionRationale.primaryReason: Leading the lowest card (spade 3) is the least committal opening play. It disposes of a weak card, keeps 4/5/6 available, and lets me observe responses before committing better cards.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: Higher than the 3 and slightly more useful later; prefer to lead the minimum.
A middle low card that is better saved for a future play or to cover a slightly higher lead.
The highest card in hand; playing it now would waste the most strength on a weak opening lead.

risks: Any opponent with a card higher than 3 or any trump can win the trick.
Partner may not be able to take the lead if their cards are also low.
riskAssessment.mitigation: Committing only the 3 minimizes the cost of losing the trick and keeps the remaining spades for future rounds.
